assertions.text: Vehicle accelerated for about ten seconds after pulling away.
Vehicle reached roughly 45 km/h before the impact.
The vehicle was accelerating when the impact occurred.
After the collision, the vehicle slowed and rolled to a near stop.
No explicit braking prior to impact is claimed (only accelerating).
The pull away (start of motion) occurred immediately before the ten-second acceleration period.

verdicts.evidence: [accelerating for about ten seconds after pulling away](T2) detection shows a sustained acceleration segment from [t_start 10.41 to t_end 11.27](T2) but note the window start alignment; GPS speed rises from [first_value 0.0 at t=0.0 to max 47.0 km/h](T3) over the window which is consistent with ~10 s of acceleration.
[gps_speed max 47.0 km/h](T3) is within ~10% of the claimed [roughly 45 km/h](T2) and a [speed_drop detection lists from_kmh 45.0 before the drop](T2).

Two high-magnitude impact spikes are detected but they occur at the same time: [accel_x impact peak_t 11.01 magnitude 5.86 g](T2) and [accel_y impact peak_t 11.01 magnitude 2.67 g](T2), consistent with a single impact event registered across axes.
The device recorded a large peak acceleration of [5.862 g at t=11.01 s on accel_x](T3) which meets the detector threshold and supports the claim the impact was heavy.

A sustained acceleration segment is detected that immediately precedes the impact ([t_start 10.41 to t_end 11.27](T2)), and GPS speed was at [from_kmh 45.0 at t~10–11 before dropping](T2), supporting that the vehicle was accelerating at impact.

GPS speed dropped from [45.0 km/h at t_start 10.0 to 22.0 km/h at t_end 13.0](T2) and the last recorded GPS sample is [2.0 km/h at t=16.0](T3), supporting that after collision the vehicle slowed and rolled to a near stop.

The narrative claims accelerating with no explicit braking; brake channel data are not present in the window info and detect_events did not report brake transitions, so absence cannot be confirmed from the available sensors.

Timing of the "pull away" (start of motion) relative to the ten-second acceleration is a narrative detail; GPS shows motion starting at [first_value 0.0 at t=0.0 and speed rising to max 47.0 km/h](T3) but the exact claim that pull-away occurred immediately before the ten-second period cannot be determined from the sampled channels.

result.rationale: Telemetry shows the vehicle accelerated to about [47.0 km/h max](T3), an impact spike at [t=11.01 s with 5.862 g peak on accel_x](T3)/(T2), and a post-impact speed drop from [45.0 to 22.0 km/h between t=10.0–13.0](T2) then to [2.0 km/h by t=16.0](T3). These data support the main narrative points; brake channel and precise timing of pull-away are not available so two assertions are unverifiable.

citations.claim: accelerating for about ten seconds after pulling away
first_value 0.0 at t=0.0 to max 47.0 km/h
speed_drop detection lists from_kmh 45.0 before the drop
5.862 g at t=11.01 s on accel_x
45.0 km/h at t_start 10.0 to 22.0 km/h at t_end 13.0
first_value 0.0 at t=0.0 and speed rising to max 47.0 km/h

result.notes: 100 Hz IMU over a 16 s window. Units: accel g, gyro deg/s, speed km/h. Device-to-vehicle axis alignment is NOT documented; do not assume which axis is longitudinal without cross-checking against GPS speed.

detections.note: direction ambiguous: device axis orientation undocumented

result.thresholds_doc: impact spike: |a-baseline|>2.5g (>=20ms, merged<0.25s); sustained: 0.5s-avg in 0.35-1.2g; rotation: >40.0deg/s for 0.3s; net rotation >60.0deg; speed drop: >=15.0km/h in <=3.0s; brake: 0->1 transitions; CISS impact: max |crash-pulse delta-V|

accel_x.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

accel_y.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

accel_z.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned
